quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hungarian paprika, one of Hungary's most famous exports, comes in eight different grades ranging from delicate and mild to hot and spicy. The town of Szeged is so renowned for its paprika production that it has a whole museum dedicated to this beloved red spice!

short_read.title: How Hummingbirds Navigate Thousands of Miles
short_read.teaser: These tiny birds, weighing less than a nickel, can remember every flower they've visited and return to feeding spots year after year. Scientists have discovered their remarkable memory helps them migrate from Mexico to the Pacific Northwest each spring.

medium_read.title: The Secret Lives of Olympic Peninsula's Black Bears
medium_read.teaser: Olympic National Park is home to a thriving black bear population that spends much of summer feasting on berries and salmon. This beautiful article explores their daily routines, family bonds, and how they prepare for winter hibernation.

long_read.title: The Hungarian Revolution of 1956: Personal Stories
long_read.teaser: This moving collection captures the voices of ordinary Hungarians who lived through the 1956 uprising, sharing stories of courage, hope, and resilience. Their personal accounts bring to life a pivotal moment in Hungarian history with warmth and humanity.

port_angeles.title: Seal Pups at Ediz Hook
port_angeles.description: July is a wonderful time to spot harbor seal pups along Ediz Hook! Mother seals often rest with their pups on the sandy beaches, teaching them to swim and hunt. Remember to enjoy them from a distance of at least 100 yards to keep both you and the seals safe.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats about ___ times per minute while hovering.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Amazing! You're absolutely right! That tiny heart works incredibly hard to power those rapid wings.
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! The answer is 1,200 beats per minute — that's about 20 times per second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Those tiny hearts are amazing — they beat about 1,200 times per minute while hovering! That's what powers their incredible flying abilities.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To put this in perspective, a hummingbird's heart rate can slow to about 250 beats per minute when they rest at night, entering a hibernation-like state called torpor to conserve energy.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is indeed butterfly — such a lovely, musical word!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó' — doesn't it sound beautiful? (Madár means bird, virág means flower, and napfény means sunshine.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: The word 'pillangó' comes from the verb 'pillan' meaning 'to glance' or 'to flutter,' perfectly capturing the delicate, fleeting movement of butterfly wings. Hungarian is full of such poetic word origins!

puzzle_3.question: Which sea creature is known to live for over 200 years in the cold waters of the Pacific Northwest?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Yes! Geoduck clams can live over 200 years — they're among the longest-living animals on Earth and are found right here in the Strait of Juan de Fuca!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's the geoduck clam! These remarkable creatures can live over 200 years, making them one of the longest-living animals on our planet. They burrow deep in the sand of local waters.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The oldest geoduck ever recorded was 168 years old, but scientists believe many live well past 200 years. Their long necks, or siphons, can extend up to three feet to reach the water's surface while their bodies stay safely buried.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
